codeReview.summary: The feature delivers a robust X25519-based 1:1 key exchange module with persisted session metadata, explicit group/channel E2EE rejection, and comprehensive targeted tests covering happy paths and failure modes. Core requirements are met for this foundation stage.

issues.description: KeyExchangeNotCompleteError tells users to run `mors key-exchange`, but the CLI usage and command dispatch do not currently expose a `key-exchange` command, so the remediation guidance is currently not executable.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mors-global-worker` skill requires manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks, but this feature handoff only reports automated `commandsRun` while still marking `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`. The skill/procedure contract should clarify when manual checks are optional for pure library-level features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:31 requires manual checks; mission handoff file 2026-02-28T11-31-13-074Z__e2ee-key-exchange-and-1to1-scope-enforcement__6c9ca849-fa56-4eb9-8ea4-32339113eee6.json contains `verification.commandsRun` but no manual/interactive check section.

summary: Reviewed commit 857c88ad in depth (diff, tests, handoff, transcript skeleton, and skill context). Implementation satisfies feature intent for 1:1 key exchange and unsupported group/channel enforcement, with one non-blocking UX guidance mismatch in error messaging.
